title: Cache Adapters
description: Memory, Redis, Deno KV, and tiered caching

blocks.text: Howl's cache adapters implement a simple interface used for both API response caching and rate limit counters. You can plug in any backend by implementing three methods.
In-memory LRU cache. Zero dependencies, instant access. Not shared across instances.
memoryCache is perfect for development and single-instance production deployments. For multi-instance, use it as the fast tier in tryCache.
Redis-backed cache shared across all instances. Requires an ioredis-compatible client.
Deno KV-backed cache. On Deno Deploy, KV is globally consistent and shared across all edge instances automatically. Locally, it uses SQLite per-process.
kvCache uses Deno KV's native expireIn option — no background cleanup jobs needed. On Deno Deploy this is the preferred adapter.
Chains two adapters. Reads from primary; if unavailable or too slow, falls back to the secondary. Writes go to both.
If Redis goes down, tryCache automatically uses the in-memory fallback after a timeout, keeping your app alive.
Implement CacheAdapter to use any storage backend — PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, Cloudflare KV, etc.
